data.description:
## Why
On a Jira hard-delete the reconciler preserves local content (deliberate, per ADR 0027/0028) BUT never re-creates the Jira issue — the `follow_on` re-create payload is built and never consumed, and the branch writes a COMMENT claiming "outbound re-create follow-on emitted" that is false. Investigation confirmed this is an UNINTENTIONAL GAP (docstring/comment/TODO all state re-create as intended; no ADR/ticket records a decision to drop it; the tracking epic `3e36` was lost when the store was recreated at extraction). Maintainer chose to FINISH the feature.

Citations: `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/apply_inbound.py:541-560` (hard_delete branch, TODO(epic-3e36) at :560); `applier.py:469-471` (follow_on consumer only handles `kind=="suppress_pair"`); `reconcile.py:68-79` (dispatch table has no `create_after_hard_delete` entry); prior audit flag `.joe-janitor/report-2026-07-02.md:137`.

## What
Wire the hard-delete re-create end-to-end, with care (this touches LIVE Jira writes):
1. Fix the key mismatch: `_apply_inbound_delete` selects the branch on `payload.get("probe_outcome")` but the prod probe router emits `reason` — so the hard_delete branch is currently unreachable outside the unit test. Reconcile the keys.
2. Route `create_after_hard_delete` through the EXISTING `_apply_outbound_create` path (add a dispatch entry that delegates) so it inherits dedup-aware create + binding persistence — do not build a parallel create path.
3. Idempotency: ensure a pass dying after the Jira create but before binding persistence does NOT double-create on retry (interacts with binding_store recovery).
4. Guard against resurrecting intentional deletes (only genuine hard_delete, not redirect/out_of_window/trash).
5. Fix the misleading COMMENT regardless.

## Scope
Reconciler inbound hard-delete -> outbound re-create. Recreate a tracking epic to replace the lost `3e36` reference.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Key mismatch fixed so the hard_delete branch is reachable in the real dispatch path
- [ ] `create_after_hard_delete` routes through the existing outbound-create path (dedup + binding persistence)
- [ ] Idempotent: retry after partial failure does not double-create the Jira issue (test)
- [ ] Only genuine hard-deletes trigger re-create (redirect/out_of_window/trash do not)
- [ ] The branch COMMENT accurately reflects what happened
- [ ] End-to-end test: inbound hard-delete -> outbound re-create in the same pass
data.title: Finish Jira hard-delete -> outbound re-create (replaces lost epic-3e36)
